purpose: Preserve and extend the shared Claude, Codex, ChatGPT and Git operating model so future work can resume from visible progress, improve quality, reduce collisions and support larger ambitions without creating unnecessary platform complexity.

growth_principle.statement: Bastian is not limited to small phases. The project may pursue large, ambitious outcomes. Phase size must adapt to risk, reversibility, evidence and coordination needs rather than being treated as a fixed personal preference.
growth_principle.operating_rule: Use the largest coherent scope that remains auditable and safely reversible. Split work only when it reduces collision, uncertainty or rollback risk.
growth_principle.anti_pattern: Do not encode 'Bastian prefers small phases' as a permanent memory or restriction.
growth_principle.desired_outcome: Increase the scale of what can be built while improving control, evidence, agent coordination and learning speed.

roles.Bastian: Final authority, business semantics, risk acceptance and model/reasoning selection in the UI.
roles.ChatGPT: Architecture, synthesis, prompt governance, cross-agent coordination and direction review.
roles.Claude: Independent research, semantic challenge, platform experimentation and adversarial validation.
roles.Codex: Repository-grounded implementation, deterministic validation, DB-aware execution under explicit authorization and memory promotion.
roles.Git: Shared evidence, handoff and durable progress bus.

blind_routing.rule: Model and reasoning recommendations are shown to Bastian outside the agent prompt. The executing agent receives the task contract without being predisposed by the selected model or expected conclusion.

bayesian_direction.current_action: Collect comparable load observations before deterministic scorecards or probabilistic models.

implemented: Path-scoped Control Gestion and research-memory rules
Local Claude auto-memory with private user/project pointers
pending: Use /sz-load-observation once on a completed real load before adding more skills
Resume Claude CG005F revalidation after usage limits reset

implemented: Guarded no-write defaults and exact staged scopes
Independent validation and Git evidence flow
Control Gestion canonical builder and guarded route replacement implementation
pending: Versioned local environment and safe task actions
Codex repository skills that reuse deterministic project scripts
Minimal project rules and review gates

shared_memory.principle: Private agent memory may improve local continuity, but cross-agent-critical facts, decisions and evidence must remain in Git-visible research artifacts.

roadmap.name: Worktree Isolation and Local Environment
roadmap.objective: Allow concurrent implementation, review and platform work without contaminating the primary checkout or repeating git_worktree_not_clean as normal operating state.
scope: Create a versioned Codex local environment/setup contract
Add safe actions for preflight, tests, status and dry-runs
Document handoff and cleanup without deleting unmerged work
success_metrics: Two concurrent phases can run without overlapping modified files
No accidental staging of another phase
Fresh worktree reaches test-ready state reproducibly
dependencies: Finish the active RUTA_RUTERO validation/correction chain or isolate it first

roadmap.name: First Real Load Observation Use
roadmap.objective: Prove that /sz-load-observation reduces manual work and preserves privacy using one completed RUTA_RUTERO or KPIONE2 load.
scope: Validate the skill independently with Codex
Generate one UNREVIEWED candidate from a safe phase JSON
Append to the ledger only in a separately authorized phase
success_metrics: No secrets, PII, payloads or row collections
Ledger is not written by the skill
Manual preparation effort is measurably reduced

roadmap.name: Shared Codex Skills over Existing Deterministic Scripts
roadmap.objective: Expose existing project tooling through small Codex skills without duplicating business logic.
candidate_skills: sz-preflight selecting an existing approved preflight profile
constraints: No duplicate implementation of script logic
No automatic DB, loader, commit or push authorization
success_metrics: Same deterministic output as direct script execution

roadmap.name: Cross-Agent Response Envelope and Drift Diagnostics
roadmap.objective: Normalize observable execution facts while keeping quality, confidence and verdict explicitly self-assessed until independently validated.
scope: Stable types for files, DB access, writes, apply, commit and push
Detect horizon, backlog, capability and memory divergence
Do not force every task into an oversized schema
success_metrics: Self-assessment and independent validation remain distinct

roadmap.name: Minimal Rules and Native Review Gate
roadmap.objective: Reduce high-confidence command risks and make native review a repeatable gate without blocking legitimate authorized work.
scope: Forbid git add . and git add -A
Prompt on destructive or write-capable commands
Use native review before merge or production-facing apply
Test every matcher with positive and negative cases
non_goals: Treat rules as a complete security boundary

roadmap.name: Diagnostic Hooks and Context Observability
roadmap.objective: Observe loaded instructions, context drift and out-of-scope changes before introducing enforcement hooks.
constraints: No DB or production side effects

roadmap.name: MCP, SDK and CI Control Plane
roadmap.objective: Evolve the operating model into a controlled AI development plane only after worktree isolation, deterministic skills and observation contracts are proven.
candidate_capabilities: Read-only STOCK_ZERO MCP with allowlisted tools
entry_conditions: Proven value from earlier platform phases

progress_measurement.principle: Platform improvement is accepted only when it reduces errors, collisions, rework, prompt size or elapsed time without weakening control.
metrics: time from task start to validated commit
number of skills used at least once versus unused skills
manual steps removed by deterministic tooling
false positives from rules or hooks
progress_measurement.review_rule: Do not add the next platform layer until the previous layer has been used in real work and its benefit or failure is recorded.

store_in_auto_memory: Private preferences and local continuity that are not authoritative for other agents
never_encode_as_personal_limit: A past workflow preference is a permanent capability ceiling

immediate_next_actions: Complete or resume Claude CG005F after the session limit resets.
Continue the RUTA_RUTERO path toward local PostgreSQL validation before any Supabase DDL or apply.
Run Codex independent validation of commit 9f19cca for /sz-load-observation.
After current operational work is isolated or closed, begin CODEX_PLATFORM_001 worktree and local-environment design.
Use this file as the progress checkpoint before creating additional skills, hooks, subagents, MCP servers or automations.
global_non_goals: No automatic model selection in the UI
No skill proliferation without demonstrated recurrence
No subagents solely because the feature exists
No replacement of human authority with memory, rules, hooks or probability
review_triggers: Completion of the active RUTA_RUTERO local-lab chain
Two concurrent Codex/Claude phases collide in the primary checkout
A new model or platform capability materially changes available workflows
Bastian changes product direction or authorizes a larger implementation horizon
